description_long: This church is constructed primarily from light tan stone blocks, featuring a prominent square bell tower with battlements at the top. The main body of the church has a pitched roof covered in weathered gray tiles, and the side walls are lined with large arched windows, some of which appear to contain stained glass. The entrance is marked by a small gabled porch, and the structure is supported by several buttresses along its length. The bell tower includes a decorative window with colored glass and is integrated into the main building, rising above the nave. The overall design is simple yet robust, with architectural elements reminiscent of traditional English parish churches.
description: A stone church with a square bell tower, arched stained glass windows, and a pitched gray roof, supported by buttresses. The entrance features a small gabled porch.
description_view_0: Side view showing the length of the nave, multiple arched windows, and the full height of the bell tower.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the back of the bell tower, buttresses, and the absence of windows on this side.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the entrance porch visible, arched windows, and the bell tower's decorative window.
description_view_3: Front view showing the main entrance with a gabled porch and a large arched window above the door.
